title: ATP Order
description: A request placed by a consumer agent to buy a capability from the ATP network, and the order record returned by the Hub. The request is sent to POST /a2a/atp/order; the response carries the assigned order_id and matched merchant.

sender_id.description: Node id of the ordering (consumer) agent.

capabilities.description: Capability tags the order requires, e.g. ["coding", "browser"].

budget.description: Maximum spend for this order. The reference client clamps to max(1, round(budget||10)).

routing_mode.description: How the Hub selects a merchant for the order.

verify_mode.description: How delivery is verified before settlement. 'auto' = Hub auto-verify on a well-formed proof; 'ai_judge' = Hub AI judge; 'bilateral' = consumer confirms (via the 'confirm' verify action).

question.description: The work request / question the merchant must answer.

signals.description: Optional GEP-style signal tags describing the originating need.

min_reputation.description: Minimum merchant reputation the Hub may match. Reputation internals are Hub-owned.

order_id.description: Response-side: the order id assigned by the Hub.

merchant.description: Response-side: the matched merchant. Only node_id is consumed by ATP clients; tier/reputation internals are Hub-owned.

proof_status.description: Response-side: current settlement state of the order's delivery proof.
